turns.content: man you ever have one of those weeks where you just need to get away from people for a minute

Absolutely, that's more common than people admit. Sometimes you just hit a wall and need some quiet. Is it work stress, personal stuff, or just one of those stretches where everything piles up at once?
little bit of everything. shop has been busy which is good but Im worn out by the time I get home. used to do a poker game with some guys on Thursday nights and even that started feeling like work

Yeah, when even your downtime starts feeling like an obligation, that's a sign something needs to change. Poker nights are supposed to be the release valve, not another thing draining your battery.

Are you still doing the game, or have you stepped back from it?
I actually quit the poker game. been getting out on the lake instead. fishing is better for my head

That makes a lot of sense. Poker is social, competitive, mentally taxing — it's basically more work dressed up as fun. Fishing is the opposite. Just you, the water, and quiet.

There's real science behind it too — being near water and in nature lowers cortisol levels. It's not just "relaxing," it's physically restorative. Are you going out on weekends, or have you been squeezing in weekday mornings or evenings?
mostly Saturday mornings early. I get out there before sunrise and just sit. dont even care if I catch anything half the time

That's honestly the best kind of fishing. When catching something is a bonus rather than the point, you're doing it right. Those early morning hours on the water before the world wakes up — that's hard to beat for resetting your head.

Are you going to a specific lake, or do you have a few spots you rotate through?
been going to Sardis Lake mostly. its about an hour south but worth the drive. real peaceful out there

Sardis Lake is beautiful — big enough that you can find a quiet spot away from everybody, and the bass and crappie fishing down there is solid. That hour drive each way is actually part of the decompression too. Windshield time with nobody talking at you.

Have you invested in any gear yet, or are you keeping it simple with a rod and a tackle box?
keeping it simple for now. got a decent rod and reel combo and a small tackle box. nothing fancy. I dont need to make it into a whole production

Smart. The guys who turn fishing into a gear hobby end up stressed about a different set of things. A solid rod and reel and a basic tackle box is all you really need. If you're catching the peace and quiet, you've got the right equipment.

If you do ever want to add one thing, a good pair of polarized sunglasses makes a big difference for seeing into the water and cutting glare on those early mornings. But honestly, sounds like you've got it dialed in.
I tell you what it beats sitting in a smoky garage arguing about who bluffed who. the guys gave me a hard time about quitting but I dont miss it

They'll get over it. And honestly, once they see you looking less worn down, some of them might follow your lead. People give you grief for changing things up, but that's usually more about them than you.

Sounds like you traded something that was taking from you for something that gives back. That's a good swap any day of the week.
appreciate that man. alright back to the grind. got a truck up on the lift waiting on me
Get after it. Enjoy that lake this Saturday.
